items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: The council held a study session to examine planned infrastructure and facility projects for the upcoming fiscal year. This is the opportunity to learn what physical improvements are coming to Mountain View.

items.headline: City Council introduces new tenant protections ordinance
items.summary: Sunnyvale is amending its residential tenant protections program through a new ordinance. The specifics of what protections are being added or changed will be determined as the ordinance moves through the approval process.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan Status Report
items.summary: The City Council reviewed progress on a new Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan. This plan will shape how San José's library system evolves, affecting service quality and facility improvements residents use.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets bonus floor space for upgrades
items.summary: The building at 405 Kipling Street is being elevated from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, earning 5,000 square feet of additional floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation work.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A rezoning proposal would allow an eight-story building with 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and commercial space on San Antonio Road. The project requires City Council approval and represents significant new housing in the area.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for Wednesday, April 8 was cancelled and rescheduled to Wednesday, April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street that was up for review will be re-noticed with a new hearing date, meaning any neighbors or residents tracking this development will need to watch for updated notices.

items.headline: City approves construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully specified in the available agenda text. Residents may want to follow up on what construction project this involves and where it will occur in Los Gatos.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project moves forward
items.summary: The City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project. This infrastructure project addresses traffic and rail safety at a key South Bay corridor.
